title: The Florence flood and 20 years after - UPI Archives
highlights: the treasure damaged or destroyed: Etruscan art, ancient armor, musical instruments, priceless Renaissance art and architecture. Thousands of flood-damaged art works have been restored. Two decades later, thousands more still await restoration. The single greatest loss, art historians agree, was to Cimabue's crucifix, painted on wood around 1280. It had been moved from an upper floor of the Uffizi Galleries to the museum at the Church of Santa Croce, which was flooded to a level of 13 feet.
